pi_items.source: Analysis of slp.mon.ltm.nc via netCDF4 shows the true minimum mean SLP in January in the Northern Hemisphere is 993.88 hPa (Icelandic Low).
The true minimum mean SLP is a single value of 993.88 hPa. The Aleutian Low's minimum is 997.56 hPa, meaning the author's expected range of 997-1002 is too high for the deepest system.
Aviation International Standard Atmosphere (ISA) defines standard sea-level pressure as 1013.25 hPa, which is commonly rounded to 1013 hPa.
Standard aviation rule of thumb states that 1 hPa deviation equals approximately 30 feet of altitude error.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real NCEP Reanalysis long-term mean sea-level pressure (SLP) data. Analysis of slp.mon.ltm.nc confirms the deepest low-pressure system in the Northern Hemisphere in January is the Icelandic Low, with a minimum SLP of 993.88 hPa. The Aleutian Low is secondary at 997.56 hPa. The author's claimed expected range of 997-1002 hPa is contradicted because the actual minimum is ~994 hPa. The standard pressure of 1013 hPa and the 30 ft/hPa rule of thumb are verified standard aviation constants.
